retronator_pixelartacademy-learnmode.js|: Learn pixel art fundamentals in the educational game by Retronator.

I'm planning to add more elements of art during Early Access.

Until then, focus just on the lines and shapes.
This will build your foundation before tackling harder elements such as values and colors.

You can now create your own artworks in the drawing app.

This feature is very barebones and isn't meant to replace dedicated drawing software.
You can use it to further explore pixel art evaluation, but note that the evaluation itself is very experimental
and only works on lines for now.

Your to-do list is empty!

Use the Study Plan app when you're ready to achieve new goals.

You completed all the tasks there are in the game so far.
I hope you had fun and learned something along the way!

While the game is in Early Access, the goal is to continually add new content.
Check back soon.

Please write a Steam review if you can, it would help immensely.
Thank you so much!
retronator_pixelartacademy-learnmode-int: Pixel art tools
Tutorial
Click here to read task instructions

This notepad will keep track of your current tasks.
You can always click on a task to learn how to complete it.

Click on the arrow to get back to the tasks.
Learn about to-do tasks

The notepad on the main screen keeps track of your current tasks.
You can always click on a task to learn how to complete it.

Click on the notebook below to see your to-do tasks.

And click on me anytime to hear my thoughts!
Learn basic pixel art tools

In the Drawing app, complete the Basics tutorial to
learn how to use essential drawing tools.
Learn about helper tools

In the Drawing app, complete the Helpers
tutorial to get used to extra tools such as zooming and drawing lines.
Learn about switching colors

In the Drawing app, complete the Colors
tutorial to learn how to switch between different colors.
Complete the pixel art software challenge

In the Drawing app under the Challenges section, select a pixel art
sprite and copy it to show you got the hang of using pixel art software.

You can complete to-do tasks in any order you want!

Use the Study Plan to explore your choices.
Play the game

In the PICO-8 app, try out the game Snake. Notice the art assets included (green snake and brown food).
Score some points to continue.
Draw Snake sprites

In the Drawing app, find the Snake art assets in the Projects section.
Redraw the sprites for the snake body and the food piece.
See sprites in action

With the game sprites replaced, run PICO-8 again and see how your art looks in the game. Do any updates until
you're happy. Score some more points to complete the project.
Learn essential pixel art tools and create art for your first game.
retronator_pixelartacademy-learnmode-des: In the Drawing app, complete the Shape language tutorial to learn what characteristics basic shapes convey.
In the Pixeltosh app, open the Invasion drive and open the Invasion Design Document file.

In the PICO-8 app, run the first build of Invasion.
Notice the first art asset in the game and imagine how you want it to look.
After adding the Defender entity in the Invasion Design Document, go to the Drawing app and complete the sprite for the player unit.

After adding the Defender projectile entity in the Invasion Design Document,
go to the Drawing app and complete the sprite for the projectiles you can shoot.
Optionally, change the explosion as well.

After adding the Invader entity in the Invasion Design Document,
go to the Drawing app and complete the sprite for the enemy units.

After adding the Invader projectile entity in the Invasion Design Document,
go to the Drawing app and complete the sprite for enemy projectiles.
Optionally, change the explosion as well.
With the defender and invaders shooting at each other, run Invasion on PICO-8 and complete one level of the game by destroying all the invaders.
Learn the main concepts and principles of design to intentionally shape your creations.
retronator_pixelartacademy-learnmode-pix: Pixel art fundamentals

You can now play extra music!

In the Music app, you'll find Shnabubula's piano improvisations that take me straight back to The Sims build mode.

You can now play extra music!

In the Music app, you'll find a cassette tape with vaporwave tracks by Glaciære.

Hey, I got my hands on a demo tape by HOME a.k.a. the brilliant kid who started the chillsynth genre!

You can find it in the Music app.

I got you a new cassette tape with two vaporwave albums by Glaciære.

You can find it in the Music app.
It's time to get funky! You can now play Revolution Void in the Music app.
If you want some relaxing, ambient music, I got a very long cassette tape from State Azure.

I got even more music for you, two albums from Three Chain Links.
He makes cool stuff inspired by the 80s and old video games.
Do you like chiptunes? I added some good DOS ones from Extent of the Jam to the Music app.
I got you a new cassette tape, Shnabubula's piano improvisations that take me straight back to The Sims build mode.
Learn about lines
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: line tutorial to learn about the most foundational element of art.
Learn about shapes
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: shape tutorial to learn about drawing things out of shapes.
Learn about colors
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: color tutorial to learn about giving color to shapes.
Learn about form
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: form tutorial to learn about the 3D nature of objects.
Learn about space
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: space tutorial to learn about the distribution of elements in artworks.
Learn about value
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: value tutorial to learn about shading.
Learn about texture
In the Drawing app, complete the Elements of art: texture tutorial to learn about detailed materials.
Learn about lines in pixel art
In the Drawing app, complete the Pixel art lines tutorial to learn about jaggies.
Learn about diagonals in pixel art
In the Drawing app, complete the Pixel art diagonals tutorial to learn about how different angles affect the patterns of jaggies.
Learn about curves in pixel art
In the Drawing app, complete the Pixel art curves tutorial to learn what makes lines appear smooth.
Learn about line width in pixel art
In the Drawing app, complete the Pixel art line width tutorial to learn how you can achieve different line thicknesses.
Draw fan art with pixel-perfect lines

In the Drawing app, choose a reference in the Pixel art line art challenge.
Complete the drawing, enable the Pixel-perfect lines criterion in the pixel art evaluation paper, and achieve a score of 80% or more (keep both doubles and corners as required).
Draw fan art with even diagonals

In the Drawing app, choose a reference in the Pixel art line art challenge.
Complete the drawing, enable the Even diagonals criterion in the pixel art evaluation paper, and achieve a score of 80% or more while having at least 10 lines with even segment lengths.
Draw fan art with smooth curves

In the Drawing app, choose a reference in the Pixel art line art challenge.
Complete the drawing, enable the Smooth curves criterion in the pixel art evaluation paper, and achieve a score of 80% or more (both in total and individually for abrupt length changes, straight parts, and inflection points).
Draw fan art with a consistent line width style

In the Drawing app, choose a reference in the Pixel art line art challenge.
Complete the drawing, enable the Consistent line width criterion in the pixel art evaluation paper, and achieve a score of 80% or more for either individual line width consistency or for having a uniform line width type.

Pixel art evaluation is being continually improved and is a bit of an experimental feature.

Don't take its scores too seriously. Trust your artistic judgment over directly following it.
Learn about simplification
In the Drawing app, complete the Simplification tutorial to learn how to intentionally simplify your drawings.
Draw quickly
In the Pixeltosh app, launch the game Draw Quickly and try both symbolic drawing and realistic drawing.
Learn about pixel art size
In the Drawing app, complete the Pixel art size tutorial to learn which factors go into deciding how big to make your pixel art.

The Pinball project still has many rough edges. I'll do my best to fix any breaking bugs you encounter.

I wish I had time to put in better instructions and more playfield parts as well. Let me know if you'd like that too!
Open your pinball machine
In the Pixeltosh app, open the Pinball Creation Kit drive and open the My Pinball Machine file.
Draw the ball
In the Drawing app, find the Pinball project and turn the ball sprite into a circle.
Oh no! It looks like the ball is a cube! Change it to a sphere in the Drawing app so it will roll.
Try out the new ball
Return to the pinball machine and test how the new ball moves on the playfield.
Change the playfield
In the Pinball project, draw a curve at the top of the playfield to redirect the ball from the shooter lane into the main playfield area.
Test the new playfield
Back on the Pixeltosh, plunge the ball strong enough to shoot it around the newly drawn curve.
Draw the gobble hole
In the Pinball project, redraw the Gobble hole sprite to any shape you want.
Score some points
Use the Edit mode of Pinball Creation Kit to place a gobble hole (or more) on the playfield and play until you get some points on the scoreboard.
Add pins

Add pins to your playfield to make the ball's trajectory more interesting.
You can do this in two ways. In Edit mode, drag individual pins onto the playfield.
Alternatively, draw 1×1 or 2×2 pixel dots directly on the Playfield sprite.
Draw the drain

Similar to the gobble hole, the drain is an area that catches the ball, except it scores no points.
You can use it as an additional hole shape that usually appears at the bottom of the playfield.
Add the drain

Place the drain onto the playfield.
Additionally, you can redraw the playfield to guide the ball to the drain at the bottom.
Draw a bumper
Draw a design for the Bumper sprite. A spring will be placed along the outline to bounce the ball away.
Place bumpers on the playfield

Make some space on the playfield by removing parts if needed.
Place multiple bumpers and adjust the bounciness of their springs on the Settings tab.
Draw a gate

To prevent the ball from returning to the shooter lane, we'll need a gate.
In the Pinball project, modify the Gate sprite as desired.
Add a gate to the shooter lane
Place the gate at the exit of the shooter lane and rotate it so the ball can go out but not in.
Remove gobble holes

The time of mechanical pinball machines is coming to an end.
With new ways to score points, remove the gobble holes from the playfield to make way for flippers.
Draw a flipper
Flippers have arrived! Draw a desired shape for the left flipper as it will appear in its resting state.
Play with flippers

Add two flippers at the bottom of the playfield. Use the Edit menu to flip a left flipper into a right one.
On the Settings tab, adjust the angle range to suit your flipper.
Modernize the lower third

With flippers in your arsenal, draw a more modern layout for the lower third of your playfield.
A typical arrangement has outer and inner lanes, as well as slingshots.
Edit the Playfield sprite until you are happy with how the design plays.
Give bumpers some kick

You can now turn static bumpers into active ones.
Select a bumper you placed on the playfield and click on the Settings tab in the editor.
Click on the active option to turn it into a bumper that will forcefully kick the ball away from it, increasing the game's excitement.
If you want, use this opportunity to update your bumper drawing as well.
Streamline the upper third

On your Playfield sprite, use smooth curves to draw lanes along which the ball can travel across the upper part of the playfield.
Aim the lane entrances and exits in the direction of the flippers.
The upper third also usually provides a place for multiple bumpers to kick the ball between them.
Draw a spinning target
Draw a design for the Spinning target sprite. You can also adjust its size as desired.
Get the target spinning

Add a spinning target or multiple of them to your playfield.
Set the points based on the difficulty of hitting them and play the game to rank up a lot of points.
Learn fundamental pixel art concepts such as jaggies, aliasing, and dithering.
retronator_pixelartacademy-pixelpad.js||: It's your morphing handheld computer, the latest PixelPad!
It's where you can launch apps on the PixelPad.
retronator_pixelartacademy-pixelpad-draw: It's the app for drawing pixel art.
Create a new artwork to draw anything you desire.
Import an artwork that was created with external software.

The artwork requires restricted colors.
          This limitation will be removed in the future.
retronator_pixelartacademy-pixelpad-musi: The music system for playing tapes collected during gameplay.
Play cassette tapes for extra music!
retronator_pixelartacademy-pixelpad-pixe: It's a mini version of the Macintosh 128K!
retronator_pixelartacademy-pixelpad-todo: Add new goals in the Study Plan to get more tasks.
retronator_pixelartacademy-pixelpad-stud: An app to design your learning curriculum.
Clear the checkmark to reactivate the goal and see its tasks in the Drawing app again.
Mark the goal as completed to hide its tasks from the Drawing app and make space for new goals.
Reach the flagpole to be able to mark this goal as completed.
Remove the goal from the study plan if you're not happy with its position.
Remove the goal from the study plan if you don't want to work on it anymore.
